summary: This is a brief link post directing readers to two of O'Neil's Bloomberg columns. The first examines the dystopian implications of algorithmic price discrimination, while the second analyzes Snap's business model and its need to deeply understand user psychology. The post itself contains no substantive analysis, serving purely as a pointer to external content.
key_points: Links to Bloomberg column on algorithmic price discrimination
Links to Bloomberg column on Snap's data collection imperatives
Both pieces appear to continue O'Neil's theme of critiquing how companies exploit personal data
key_insight: This link post highlights O'Neil's ongoing Bloomberg coverage of algorithmic exploitation and corporate data practices.
